perturbation.original_text: Subject to Section 5, each Company agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Company, such Company will not, and it will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Section 5, each Company agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Company, such Company will not, and it will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof), unless it is to a potential funding partner.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'unless it is to a potential funding partner', a new exception is created that allows for the disclosure of information to external parties. This contradicts the initial part of the sentence, which restricts the disclosure of any information to 'any other Person' without prior written consent. This weakens the NDA and potentially exposes sensitive information to third parties without the provider's explicit approval. This makes it contradictory in the case that, it is still considered as disclosing to any other person.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Discussions . Subject to Section 5, each Company agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other Company, such Company will not, and it will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each company will not disclose information to 'any other Person'. The changed text adds an exception: 'unless it is to a potential funding partner'. This directly contradicts the original restriction by allowing disclosure to a specific type of 'other Person', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the contradicted text states that disclosure to any other person is not allowed.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Recipient will, within one year from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider, so long as they are employed by the Provider and for three months after they cease to be employed by Provider. A Recipient is not prohibited from soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at (i) any particular individual or (ii) the employees of the Provider generally.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Recipient will, within one year from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider, so long as they are employed by the Provider and for three months after they cease to be employed by Provider. However, the Recipient may solicit any employee who has been employed by the Provider for less than 6 months. A Recipient is not prohibited from soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at (i) any particular individual or (ii) the employees of the Provider generally.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of 'However, the Recipient may solicit any employee who has been employed by the Provider for less than 6 months' directly contradicts the initial restriction on soliciting employees for one year. This creates an exception for employees with short tenure, generating uncertainty about the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause for specific employees. This contradict the one year restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Solicitation/Employment . Neither Recipient will, within one year from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider, so long as they are employed by the Provider and for three months after they cease to be employed by Provider. A Recipient is not prohibited from soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at (i) any particular individual or (ii) the employees of the Provider generally.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception that the Recipient may solicit any employee who has been employed by the Provider for less than 6 months. This directly contradicts the original restriction, which is also found in the contradicted text, stating that 'Neither Recipient will, within one year from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider'. The introduction of an exception regarding employees employed for less than 6 months generates a direct contradiction with the one-year non-solicitation clause. This creates a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the other Party, neither it nor any of its affiliates (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) or Representatives (acting in any capacity other than as an advisor in any of the following cases) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party;
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the other Party, neither it nor any of its affiliates (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) or Representatives (acting in any capacity other than as an advisor in any of the following cases) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; unless the action is necessary to prevent immediate financial loss.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless the action is necessary to prevent immediate financial loss.' introduces an exception to the standstill provision. This directly contradicts the previously stated restrictions on actions like acquisitions or mergers during the standstill period, as it allows these actions if they are deemed necessary to avoid financial loss. This creates uncertainty about the scope and enforceability of the standstill agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . Each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the other Party, neither it nor any of its affiliates (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) or Representatives (acting in any capacity other than as an advisor in any of the following cases) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party;
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'unless the action is necessary to prevent immediate financial loss' to the standstill provision in the Changed Text directly contradicts the original, more restrictive standstill provision detailed in the Contradicted Text. The original text enforces a strict six-month standstill, whereas the Changed Text introduces an exception, thereby creating a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document regarding the permitted actions during the standstill period. This adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Company understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Companies unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Company hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Companies shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Company understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Companies unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Company hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Companies shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Oral agreements are considered valid, provided that both Parties are recording it.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'Oral agreements are considered valid, provided that both Parties are recording it.' the explicit requirement for a 'final definitive agreement' is contradicted. The modified clause introduces ambiguity because while a final definitive agreement is needed to ensure no claims, there is an exception for Oral agreements provided that both Parties are recording it. This contradicts the original condition where there were no claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Not a Transaction Agreement . Each Company understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Companies unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Company hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Companies shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that no claims relating to a possible transaction exist unless a final definitive agreement has been executed. The changed text introduces an exception by stating that oral agreements are considered valid if recorded. This directly contradicts the original text, which requires a 'final definitive agreement' to avoid claims. Now, oral agreements can also lead to valid claims if recorded, which clashes with the initial condition that only a final definitive agreement ensures no claims.
